description_long: This is an electric guitar with a contoured, double-cutaway body finished in a glossy light blue color. The front features three single-coil pickups, a white pickguard, and a set of control knobs and a switch, typical of Stratocaster-style guitars. The neck is made of a lighter wood, likely maple, with a rosewood or similar fingerboard and dot inlays. The headstock is angled and fitted with six inline tuning pegs, and the back of the guitar shows a rectangular electronics cover plate. The bridge is a classic tremolo style, and the overall construction suggests a solid body. The guitar's design and hardware are consistent with popular models used in urban music scenes.
description: A glossy light blue, double-cutaway electric guitar with a white pickguard, three pickups, and a maple neck. It features a classic tremolo bridge and six inline tuning pegs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the light blue body, white pickguard, three pickups, control knobs, and the full maple neck with headstock.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the guitar's body thickness, the contour of the top, and the inline arrangement of the tuning pegs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid blue back, electronics cover plate, and the full length of the neck and headstock.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view emphasizing the body contour, back plate, and the transition between the neck and body.
attribute_headstock_design: Six inline tuning pegs, slightly curved headstock, no visible logo
